{{Quote|Some of us don't consider loyalty to be outmoded.|Virgilio to Borsk Fey'lya|<ref name="DFR">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref>}}
'''Sarin Virgilio''' was a [[Masterra]]n [[Human/Legends|Human]] starship officer of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Biography==
Virgilio was a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Prior to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] he served as third officer on a [[DP20 gunship/Legends|Corellian Gunship]]. His ship rescued the six remaining members of a [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]] team that had been working in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Impressed by their bravery he became a strong supporter of the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]], and of [[Councillor/Legends|Councilor]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]].

During the [[Thrawn campaign|Thrawn crisis]], Virgilio, now promoted to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]], commanded the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Quenfis]]'', part of the [[New Republic First Fleet|Home Guard Fleet]]. Borsk Fey'lya sent him to arrest [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his companions during the [[Katana fleet]] incident. During the conflict Fey'lya was tricked into revealing his true intentions, and Virgilio proved himself loyal to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] by supporting Solo and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in the ensuing battle.<ref name="DFRSB" />

==Behind the scenes==
According to his entry in the ''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' (and republished in ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]),'' it was his ship that recovered the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] carrying the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] plans, conflicting with ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''.
